name: azure-mgmt-apimanagement-dotnet
description: Azure Resource Manager SDK for API Management in .NET. Use for MANAGEMENT PLANE operations: creating/managing APIM services, APIs, products, subscriptions, policies, users, groups, gateways, and backends via Azure Resource Manager.
capabilities: Create and configure API Management service instances with various SKU types
Manage APIs, operations, schemas, and diagnostics programmatically
Configure products, subscriptions, and access control policies
Set API policies for rate limiting, authentication, and transformation
Perform backup and restore operations for APIM services
Manage users, groups, backends, and gateway configurations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only containing code examples for Azure API Management SDK usage. Static analysis found 0 files with executable code and 0 risk patterns. All code examples demonstrate proper security practices including DefaultAzureCredential for authentication. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: Developers need to provision and configure Azure API Management services programmatically. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for managing APIM resources using the Azure.ResourceManager.ApiManagement .NET SDK.
userTitle: Manage Azure API Management with .NET SDK
limitations: Only supports management plane operations, not data plane API calls
Requires valid Azure credentials and subscription access
Service creation operations can take 30+ minutes to complete
Does not cover direct gateway endpoint invocations

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating APIM Provisioning
useCases.description: Create infrastructure automation scripts to deploy and configure API Management services across multiple environments using CI/CD pipelines.

useCases.title: Backend Developer Publishing APIs
useCases.description: Programmatically register new APIs, configure policies, and manage subscriptions when deploying microservices to Azure.

useCases.title: Platform Team Managing Multi-Tenant APIs
useCases.description: Build tooling to create products, manage access tiers, and automate subscription workflows for internal API consumers.

promptTemplates.title: Create a New APIM Service
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Azure API Management service in the East US region using the Developer SKU. Include the necessary Azure Resource Manager code with proper authentication using DefaultAzureCredential.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Setting up a new APIM instance for development

promptTemplates.title: Add API and Configure Product
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a new API to my existing APIM service and create a product called 'Premium' with subscription required. Show how to associate the API with the product.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Publishing APIs with access control

promptTemplates.title: Implement Rate Limiting Policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an API policy that implements rate limiting of 100 calls per minute and adds custom headers to requests. Show the complete XML policy structure.

promptTemplates.title: Automated Backup Solution
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a complete backup automation script that backs up the APIM service configuration to Azure Blob Storage using managed identity. Include error handling and restore capability.

bestPractices: Always use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication instead of hardcoding credentials
Use WaitUntil.Completed for operations that must finish before proceeding
Implement proper error handling for RequestFailedException with status code checks
antiPatterns: Hardcoding subscription IDs, tenant IDs, or client secrets in source code
Using WaitUntil.Started for critical operations without tracking completion status
Ignoring ARM API error responses instead of implementing proper exception handling

faq.question: What is the difference between management plane and data plane operations?
faq.answer: Management plane (this SDK) creates and configures APIM resources. Data plane makes actual API calls through your APIM gateway endpoint.

faq.question: How long does it take to create an API Management service?
faq.answer: Developer SKU typically takes 15-30 minutes. Production SKUs may take longer. Use WaitUntil.Started for non-blocking operations.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: DefaultAzureCredential supports managed identity, environment credentials, CLI credentials, and Visual Studio credentials for flexible authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this SDK to call my APIs through APIM?
faq.answer: No. This SDK manages APIM configuration. Use HttpClient or similar to call your APIM gateway endpoint for data plane operations.

faq.question: What SKU should I use for production workloads?
faq.answer: Use Basic, Standard, or Premium SKU for production. Developer SKU has no SLA and is designed for testing only.

faq.question: How do I handle long-running operations in my code?
faq.answer: Use WaitUntil.Completed for immediate tasks or WaitUntil.Started for long operations. Monitor operation status for async completion.
